ops.body: 2026-07-19 phase 1 SHIPPED as athome 94938ed8 (rebased onto the operator's f230be81): declarative [serve.modal-vllm] recipe behind a new ServeBackend protocol (LocalServeBackend/ModalServeBackend, mirroring TrainBackend's local-vs-Modal precedent) with full ManagedServer parity — programmatically enumerated 8-member contract, isinstance-asserted for both backends. Modal app ships in-package (athome/serve_modal.py): CUDA-devel image, pinned vLLM, base rev b968826d, --enable-lora unfused bf16, scale-to-zero. Review: 18 findings → 12 accepted → fixed (incl. blocker: serialized=True pickled serve() by reference; cold-start now gated by the recipe's 900s startup budget, not the local 120s; probe_all observes the hosted recipe without waking the paid GPU; consumer credential threading through llm/ocr lanes). 1411 tests green, no-live-calls proven under a socket guard. OWNER DECISION on the snapshot claim: the 'GPU memory snapshots ~70s cold start' line from the spike report exists in neither the spike artifacts nor the port — deliverable amended: cold start is bounded by the 900s startup budget for now, and memory-snapshot support becomes a measured decision in phase 3 (calibration through the live endpoint will produce the real cold-start number; add snapshots then if it's too slow). Next: phase 2, the cc-steer drafter HTTP sibling.
